mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a self-hosted AI platform designed for user-friendly interaction with various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI APIs. It is ideal for users looking to deploy AI solutions offline with extensibility and robust user management features. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and potential complexity in setup compared to managed services.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI to manage deployments easily.

```bash

curl -Lo open-webui-cli https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux && chmod +x open-webui-cli && sudo mv open-webui-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI for Linux systems.
commands: curl -Lo open-webui-cli https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux && chmod +x open-webui-cli && sudo mv open-webui-cli /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the new cluster context.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm for easy management.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> This command installs Open WebUI in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
This will remove Open WebUI and all associated resources. Be aware that any persistent data may be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> This command uninstalls the Open WebUI release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup your data, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl cp open-webui-pod:/data /backup/data

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-web-ui

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> Backup your data before upgrading.
> This command upgrades Open WebUI to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running and in the 'Running' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure you have internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Review the deployment configuration and logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the service status and ensure it is correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved API integration with OpenAI-compatible services.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n open-webui' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
